issues.objection: disagreements_among_translators in 7.21 carries an explanation that names the substantive divergence between Advaita and Vishishtadvaita readings (and where applicable, the Gaudiya register), without flattening either side. The engineering layer respects the convergence and tags STRETCHED where the verse extends beyond operational scope. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a personal divine agency (vidadhāmy aham — I make firm); engineering analog stays at structural-pattern scope. Counter-example explicitly carves out structurally-inappropriate technique-fit so the analog does not collapse into 'commit to anything.'

notes: Disagreement-explanation review for 7.21: explanation field is non-trivial and references the specific point of doctrinal divergence. PASS.
